title: Specialized Information and Referral * Mental Health Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Specialized Information and Referral * Mental Health Issues 8 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Assistive Technology Issues Blindness Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Deafness Disabilities Issues Down Syndrome Mental Health Issues Older Adult/Aging Issues Immigration Issues Abused Lesbians/Gay Individuals Abused Transgender Individuals LGBTQ2+ Issues Transgender Individuals Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES TAYLOR CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps people find and connect to community resources. Support can include obtaining a new ID, expunging records, setting up phone plans, and finding shelter or housing. Also connects individuals to mental health and addiction treatment, including Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT). When available, items like bikes, transportation, hygiene products, sleeping bags, tents, clothing, water, and food may be provided. 358 NE Marshall Avenue Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: 7 days per week 8am-6:30pm (541) 585-0008 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHARLIE HEALTH VIRTUAL TEEN INTENSIVE OUTPATIENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides virtual intensive outpatient counseling, including individual, group, and family therapy. Specialized tracks include trauma (such as survivors of sexual assault, domestic violence, and/or other complex trauma), LGBTQ2+, maternal mental health, and neurodivergent clients, including those with autism, ADHD, or other conditions, along with co-occurring mental health issues. Also provides participants with wrap-around support, including discharge planning, family support groups, and alumni support groups and events for participants. Also, provides specialized information and referrals on mental health resources. Eligibility: Ages 8-18 Hours: Intake: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Treatment: Varies by service Email (966) 206-0414 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHARLIE HEALTH VIRTUAL ADULT INTENSIVE OUTPATIENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides virtual intensive outpatient counseling, including individual, group, and family therapy. Specialized tracks include trauma (such as survivors of sexual assault, domestic violence, and/or other complex trauma), LGBTQ2+, maternal mental health, and neurodivergent clients, including those with autism, ADHD, or other conditions, along with co-occurring mental health issues. Also provides participants with wrap-around support, including discharge planning, family support groups, and alumni support groups and events for participants. Also, provides specialized information and referrals on mental health resources. Eligibility: Ages 18-64 Hours: Intake: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Treatment: Varies by service Email (986) 206-0414 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DECONSTRUCTING THE MENTAL HEALTH SYSTEM CULTURALLY SPECIFIC COMMUNITY ORGANIZATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides listings of mental health providers that identify as being a member of underserved populations. Also provides resources for providers and a therapy and wellness program. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC BRYSON CENTER ADULT THERAPY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessments, psychiatric treatment, group and individual therapy, gambling and substance use disorder treatment from counselors, social workers, and clinical psychologists. Offers case management, supportive employment services, peer support, and adult skills training for daily living with a focus on budgeting, meal prep, and using public transit. Also provides information and referrals to community resources. 200 Beatty Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Intake: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 476-2373 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC HILLSIDE CENTER ADULT THERAPY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessments, psychiatric treatment, group and individual therapy, gambling and substance use disorder treatment from counselors, social workers, and clinical psychologists. Offers case management, supportive employment services, peer support, and adult skills training for daily living with a focus on budgeting, meal prep, and using public transit. Also provides information and referrals to community resources. 1545 Harbeck Road Grants Pass, OR 97527 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Intake: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 476-2373 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC ILLINOIS VALLEY CENTER ADULT THERAPY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessments, psychiatric treatment, group and individual therapy, gambling and substance use disorder treatment from counselors, social workers, and clinical psychologists. Offers case management, supportive employment services, peer support, and adult skills training for daily living with a focus on budgeting, meal prep, and using public transit. Also provides information and referrals to community resources. 319 Caves Highway Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Intake: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 476-2373 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES SUBSTANCE ABUSE AND MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES ADMINISTRATION FINDTREATMENT.GOV Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral for mental health and substance use disorders. 5600 Fishers Lane Rockville, MD 20857 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 662-4357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HILLSBORO RECOVERY CENTER MENTAL HEALTH REFERRALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an information and referral line to help find mental health providers. County behavioral health services are provided through a network of community agencies. 5250 NE Elam Young Parkway Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Medicaid recipients and uninsured patients seeking mental health services Hours: Support Team: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 846-4528 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES TAYLOR CENTER OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC ADULT THERAPY SERVICES OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC ADULT THERAPY SERVICES OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC ADULT THERAPY SERVICES WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MENTAL HEALTH REFERRALS
